title: Asymptotic Zarankiewicz Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
context_text: Zarankiewicz and Urbanik exhibited drawings of $K_{n,n}$ achieving $Z(n,n)$ crossings; whether this is optimal remains open. The authors introduce the limiting crossing numbers $\lambda$ and $\lambda_0$ for the usual and geodesic crossing numbers, note that equation (2) gives $\lambda \leq \lambda_0 \leq \frac{1}{16}$, and phrase the conjecture that both limits equal $\frac{1}{16}$ as the asymptotic version of the classical Zarankiewicz (Turán Brickyard) problem.
paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 3) that for any nondegenerate antipodally-symmetric probability measures $\mu_1, \mu_2$ on $\mathbb{S}^2$, the edge density of the crossing graphon satisfies $t(K_2, W(\mu_1,\mu_2)) = \frac{1}{8}$, confirming that such random geodesic drawings of $K_{n,n}$ asymptotically saturate the Zarankiewicz bound; also shows (Theorems 4 and 6) that triangle densities in crossing graphons can attain any value in $\left(\frac{83}{12288}, \frac{128}{12288}\right)$, revealing rich structure beyond edge density.
notes: PDF source — the paper text is truncated mid-sentence in the bundle-crossing counting section, so later parts of the paper (if any additional conjectures appear there) could not be examined.
paper_title: Limiting crossing numbers for geodesic drawings on the sphere
